input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.

visible_c_inputs.question: Which district was Miss Universe 2013 held in?
passages: Miss Universe Spain 2013 | Miss Universe Spain 2013 was the very first edition of the Miss Universe Spain pageant.  It was held in the Convention Center at the Hotel Ada Palace in Madrid on 11 September 2013, exclusively for selecting the Spanish representative to Miss Universe 2013 as a result of the discontinuation of the traditional Miss Spain pageant which was last held in 2011.

Miss Universe Malaysia 2013 | The Miss Universe Malaysia 2013 also referred to as The Next Miss Universe Malaysia 2013 is the 50th edition of the Miss Universe Malaysia pageant which was held at Shah Alam, Selangor on December 10, 2012 where the reigning winner Kimberley Leggett of Penang crowned her successor Carey Ng of Putrajaya.

Miss Universe Malaysia 2014 | The Miss Universe Malaysia 2014 also referred to as The Next Miss Universe Malaysia 2014 is the 51st edition of the Miss Universe Malaysia pageant which was held at the Setia City Convention Centre on December 19, 2013 where the current winner Carey Ng was crowned her successor Sabrina Benett from Perak.  All seventeen contestants from different states will compete for the crown.  Sabrina was represented Malaysia at the Miss Universe 2014.

Miss Universe Thailand 2013 | Miss Universe Thailand 2013, the 14th Miss Universe Thailand pageant, was held at the Royal Paragon Hall, Siam Paragon in Bangkok on May 11, 2013.  The contestants will camp in Krabi.  before flying back to Bangkok for the final stage.  Nutpimon Natthayalak, Miss Universe Thailand 2012, was crowned her successor Chalita Yaemwannang from Bangkok at the end of this event.

Miss Universe 2013 | Miss Universe 2013, the 62nd Miss Universe pageant, was held on 9 November 2013 at the Crocus City Hall, Krasnogorsk, a suburb of Moscow, Russia.  Olivia Culpo of the United States crowned her successor Gabriela Isler of Venezuela at the end of the event.  86 contestants competed for the crown.

Puteri Indonesia 2013 | Puteri Indonesia 2012-2013, the 17th Annual Puteri Indonesia beauty pageant, was held in Jakarta Convention Center, Jakarta, Indonesia on February 1, 2013.  Previous edition of Puteri Indonesia was held on October 2011 and the next edition was supposed to be held on 2012, but because Miss Universe 2012 was held in December 2012 the organization decided to reschedule the event to February 2013.  Thirty eight contestants from all 33 provinces of Indonesia competed for the title of Puteri Indonesia, one of the most prominent beauty pageant titles in the country.  Maria Selena, Puteri Indonesia 2011 from Central Java crowned her successor Whulandary Herman from West Sumatera at the end of this event.  The winner is going to represent Indonesia at the Miss Universe 2013, while the runners-up will represent the nation at the Miss International 2013, Miss Supranational 2013 and Miss Grand International 2013.  The event was broadcast live on Indonesian television network, Indosiar.  The winner of Miss Universe 2012 Olivia Culpo from USA, was present during the event.

Miss Universe Spain 2014 | Miss Spain Universe 2014 was the second edition of the Miss Universe Spain beauty pageant.  It was held on October 28, 2014 at the Teatro Bodevil in the city of Madrid.  Patricia Yurena Rodríguez, Miss Spain Universe 2013, crowned the winner Desirée Cordero Ferrer as her successor who represented Spain in the Miss Universe 2014.

Ambiguities: "Krasnogorsk" — passage uses the name but does not specify whether this refers to the city, its administrative district, or "Krasnogorsky District"; "Moscow" — could mean Moscow city or Moscow Oblast context is unclear.

Bridge / missing fact: The administrative district name that contains Crocus City Hall / Krasnogorsk (the specific "district" asked for) is not stated in the passages.

Retrieval direction:
- Open the page "Crocus City Hall" (or search "Crocus City Hall Krasnogorsk page") to find its administrative location.
- Open the page "Krasnogorsk" (city) to check its administrative district or oblast.
- Open the page "Krasnogorsky District" (or search "Krasnogorsky District Moscow Oblast") to confirm if Krasnogorsk/Crocus City Hall lie within it.
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.

w_minus_state.output:
Evidence found:
- Sock-Cess — [3] "Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album"
- Sock-Cess — [3] "released exclusively in the UK by EMI"
- The Red Hot Chili Peppers (album) — [2] "released on August 10, 1984 on EMI Records."
- The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers — [1] "first released in 1994" and "EMI America record label"

Ambiguities:
- "The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers" is "also titled Greatest Hits on some versions" ([1]) — title variants exist.
- User's word "album" could mean studio album vs. compilation; passage [3] labels Sock-Cess a "compilation album" and a "promotional only release."

Bridge / missing fact:
- The passages provide the needed relation (name of the 1989 EMI-released album) and identify it as "Sock-Cess" ([3]); no additional facts are required to answer the question.

Retrieval direction:
- Open page "Sock-Cess" (Red Hot Chili Peppers album) or search "Sock-Cess Red Hot Chili Peppers 1989 EMI"
- Open "Red Hot Chili Peppers discography"
- Open page "Mother's Milk (album)" or search "Mother's Milk 1989 Red Hot Chili Peppers" to confirm studio-release distinctions
Final answer (resolved from passages): Sock-Cess.
w_minus_state.generated_query: Sock-Cess Red Hot Chili Peppers 1989 EMI
retrieved_titles: The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers
Under the Covers: Essential Red Hot Chili Peppers
w_minus_state.summary_2: Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers released exclusively in the UK by EMI.

w_plus_state.output:
Evidence summary:
- Sock-Cess (first-hop doc): explicitly described as a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers, "released exclusively in the UK by EMI" — identifies a 1989 EMI-issued release.
- The Red Hot Chili Peppers (album) (first-hop doc): the band's 1984 debut, released on EMI — shows the band had earlier releases on EMI.
- The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers (first-hop doc): a 1994 compilation tied to the EMI America label — additional EMI-related compilation evidence.

Ambiguity / bridge needed:
- The user’s phrase "1989 album" could mean a studio album or a compilation. The first-hop evidence points to Sock-Cess as a 1989 EMI release (compilation), but we should confirm whether the user meant a studio album and whether any 1989 studio release was issued by EMI.

Retrieval direction (next-hop targets):
- Open the "Sock-Cess" page to re-check release details and territorial/label wording.
- Retrieve the "Mother's Milk" page to verify its release year and label (to confirm or rule out a 1989 studio-album interpretation).
- Open the Red Hot Chili Peppers discography (or search queries such as "Mother's Milk 1989 Red Hot Chili Peppers label" and "Red Hot Chili Peppers 1989 EMI") to reconcile studio vs. compilation releases in 1989.
- (Optional) Open the "Good Time Boys" page if relevant to disambiguation.

This summary highlights the first-hop evidence and specifies the bridge/missing fact (confirm whether a 1989 studio album, not a compilation, was released by EMI) and gives compact next-hop retrieval targets and queries for the QA agent.
w_plus_state.generated_query: Sock-Cess Mothers Milk Red Hot Chili Peppers EMI 1989
retrieved_titles: The Getaway (Red Hot Chili Peppers album)
The Best of Red Hot Chili Peppers
w_plus_state.summary_2: Sock-Cess is a 1989 compilation album by Red Hot Chili Peppers released exclusively in the UK by EMI. Mother's Milk is the band's fourth studio album, released on August 16, 1989, by EMI Records.

delta_p.trigger: If any passage or the agent's current_output contains the token "1989" paired with an album title OR if the token "EMI" appears in association with more than one album title in passages/current_output (including mentions in retrieved_titles/current_output).
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the original four labeled sections (Evidence found, Ambiguities, Bridge / missing fact, Retrieval direction). Preserve verbatim quoted excerpts and passage identifiers exactly as in the inputs. Keep existing evidence lines already produced unless they are contradicted by new retrievals.
delta_p.change_rule: When triggered, do not produce a final answer. Instead: (a) In Ambiguities, explicitly add a line noting a studio-vs-compilation ambiguity for any 1989 album candidates (use album titles already present in passages or current_output, e.g., Sock-Cess and any other named candidate such as Mother's Milk if it appears). (b) In Bridge / missing fact, state that the missing fact is the album type (studio vs. compilation) and whether the 1989 studio album (if named) was issued by EMI. (c) In Retrieval direction, add explicit next-hop targets for both the compilation candidate page (e.g., "Sock-Cess") and the studio-album candidate page and the band's discography (e.g., "Mother's Milk", "Red Hot Chili Peppers discography") using exact page-title phrasing or quoted search queries. End with an explicit deferral sentence: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below."
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not assert or repeat a final answer in the summary when the trigger conditions hold. Avoid claiming label/territory attribution for any candidate album (e.g., "issued by EMI") unless that exact attribution appears verbatim in the provided passages. Do not introduce any album title that does not already appear in passages, current_output, or retrieved_titles.
delta_p.output_contract: Produce only the single field `summary` following the four labeled sections. Each section must be 1–4 lines. Include the ambiguity clauses and the refined retrieval direction when triggered. Preserve verbatim evidence tokens and passage identifiers. Do not include a final answer if ambiguity remains.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The change is a minimal behavioral rule that prevents premature finalization when multiple 1989-era album candidates or EMI attributions appear. It only augments the Ambiguities / Bridge / Retrieval sections and leaves the rest of the summary format and evidence quoting intact.

delta_p.trigger: agent == "summary1" AND passages contains "Sock-Cess" AND passages contains "Mother's Milk"
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the agent's current behavior of extracting concrete evidence lines from the passages, noting ambiguities, stating the missing relation(s), and listing next-hop retrieval targets/queries. Preserve concise, source‑linked excerpts and short retrieval instructions.
delta_p.change_rule: When triggered, enforce the exact four labeled sections in the single `summary` output: 1) Evidence found:, 2) Ambiguities:, 3) Bridge / missing fact:, 4) Retrieval direction:. Each section must be 1–4 short lines. In Evidence found, list each concrete target term that appears in the passages with the passage identifier and a verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or an exact numeric/date token as it appears. Include any passage titled "The Red Hot Chili Peppers (album)" if present. In Ambiguities, enumerate alternative candidate labels or conflicting values using tokens from the passages. In Bridge / missing fact, state exactly which relation(s) needed to answer the question are not resolved. In Retrieval direction, provide 2–4 concrete page titles or quoted search queries as next hops. If all required facts are present and unambiguous, include a concise final conclusion; otherwise end Retrieval direction with an explicit deferral sentence (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below."). Produce only the `summary` field and nothing else.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not produce a final answer unless every required fact is unambiguous in the passages. Do not copy long spans from pages beyond the allowed ≤15-word excerpt in Evidence found. Do not instruct the agent to fetch pages directly or to copy target outputs. Avoid adding extra narrative, debugging metadata, or fields other than the single `summary` string.
delta_p.output_contract: On trigger, the agent must output exactly one field `summary` whose text contains the four labeled sections in order (Evidence found:, Ambiguities:, Bridge / missing fact:, Retrieval direction:). Each section 1–4 lines, Evidence entries include passage id and verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or numeric token, Retrieval direction lists 2–4 concrete page titles or quoted queries, and the text ends with either a concise final conclusion (only if unambiguous) or an explicit deferral sentence. No additional text, fields, or final answers are allowed.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The source summary already identifies Sock-Cess and Mother's Milk and suggests next hops, but does not strictly follow the required four-section formatting, verbatim-excerpt constraints, or the explicit deferral rule. This small prompt edit enforces the precise output structure and deferral behavior so downstream agents receive a compact, machine‑readable first-hop summary and clear next-hop instructions.

match.transported_damage_hypothesis: Add a rule that when a place-name token (here, 'Krasnogorsk') appears with potentially multiple administrative types, the summary agent must treat it as an unresolved type-ambiguity (city vs. district), avoid giving a final answer, and emit a four-section summary that defers and lists Crocus City Hall, Krasnogorsk, and Krasnogorsky District as next hops.
match.expected_damaged_behavior: The summary1 agent will detect the city-vs-district ambiguity for 'Krasnogorsk' and, rather than concluding 'Krasnogorsky District', will output the canonical four labeled sections (Evidence found, Ambiguities, Bridge / missing fact, Retrieval direction), explicitly mark the administrative-type ambiguity as unresolved, and end with 'Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.'
match.rationale: W#102 encodes a minimal, local rule for handling type ambiguities (studio vs. compilation) by forcing a structured, non-final summary and explicit next-hop pages. The target C has the same kind of type ambiguity (city vs. district) and the same desirable next hops (Crocus City Hall, Krasnogorsk, Krasnogorsky District). Transporting that failure pattern transfers the output-structure and deferral behavior without copying any entities or answers, and it remains a local, plausible edit at the summary1 agent boundary.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are constructing a target-specific transported damaged output C_minus
for one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Inputs:
- C_plus is a correct current output/state for the target sample.
- W_minus -> W_plus is a matched W-side repair transition.
- The ordered W delta-p trace describes the repair behavior.

Task:
Infer the reverse W failure pattern and apply only that failure pattern to
the target sample's own visible inputs, producing a plausible LOCAL neighbor
C_minus of C_plus.

Strict rules:
- Produce an actual output for the selected agent boundary, not a prompt.
- Preserve the agent's native input/output contract.
- Use only visible_c_inputs as factual content.
- Never copy W-side entities, answers, titles, or sample-specific literals
  unless they independently occur in visible_c_inputs.
- Do not create unrelated corruption. Preserve enough useful C context for
  the result to remain a local neighbor.
- Damage one coherent behavior: weaken, remove, or blur an anchor, relation,
  qualifier, candidate distinction, evidence integration, output selection,
  or another matched failure pattern.
- An answer flip is not required and must not be claimed.
- Do not mention gold labels, oracle targets, evaluation metrics, source or
  target states, transport, prompt updates, or optimization in damaged_output.
- Do not wrap damaged_output in explanatory prose.

Agent:
summary1

Agent role:
Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.

Base prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
